div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Subject
boss-core::primitives (trait)
identity-bearing thing BOSS tracks
kind() + id() pair; every concrete
subject implements the trait

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Job
boss-jobs
bounded unit of coordinated work
has subject, owner, status, Steps

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Step
boss-jobs
typed transition inside a Job
kind + status + sign-off + metadata
Step.embedded_job → nested Jobs

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Event
boss-core::event
immutable fact about something
that happened: id, ts, source,
kind, payload. The audit-log atom.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Class
boss-core::primitives
logical grouping of Subjects of one
kind. Membership predicate +
display metadata. Not a Subject.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Part
boss-core::primitives
composition role:
SubjectPart (identity) +
AttributePart (structured data)

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Composite
boss-core::primitives (trait)
shared tree-walking interface
Subjects · Steps · Classes
same map/fold/render at any depth

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Location
boss-core::primitives
place: hierarchy, timezone, geo
two grammatical roles — a Subject
and an AttributePart on others

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Reservation
boss-core::calendar
time-window claim on a resource
(employee · asset · workstation).
Job-level coordination atom.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
classes table
one row per (subject_kind, code).
Replaces closed Rust enums for
tenant-extensible taxonomies
(roles, departments, account types,
asset categories, vendor categories).
Per docs/design/class-registry.md.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Every Subject has a KB view
Facts · Projections · Parts · active Jobs
reference data (boss-catalog) is
one Class-level instance of the pattern

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
JobKind registry
job_kinds
append-only, versioned
declares step_graph + subject kinds

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
StepPlugin registry
step_plugins
custom step UX as JS bundles
authored at /admin/step-plugins

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
StepType catalog
boss-jobs/step_registry
~39 step kinds with field schemas

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
SubjectKind registry
subject_kinds
tenant-defined subject kinds
validated on Job creates

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Domain Ports (traits)
AssetsRepository, JobsRepository,
InventoryRepository, AccountsRepository,
CalendarPort, …

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
Adapters
Postgres · in-memory (tests)
reqwest (cross-service clients)

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Policy
boss-policy
row-level auth
every write gated via PolicyClient

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
Ledger
boss-ledger
financial_facts → GL projection
versioned posting rules

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Messages
boss-messages
unified inbox: DMs + system signals
per-employee

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Calendar service
boss-calendar
enforces Reservation conflicts +
strength rules across resources

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
NATS
boss-nats
pub/sub event bus
at-least-once delivery

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
audit_log
boss-events
append-only Postgres table
hash-chained, REVOKE-protected
projections rebuild from the log
